protocol: Every country is scored by the cross-validation fold model that never saw it in training. Observed cells: leave-one-out (the cell is removed from its own graph and predicted from the rest). Imputable cells (is_observed=0, is_impute=1): predicted from all observed cells of that country-year.

dispersion_model.meaning: nu = alpha+beta measures ISOLATE-LEVEL HETEROGENEITY within a cell — isolates come from different hospitals, populations, months and clonal backgrounds and do not share one common resistance probability. Low nu = heterogeneous cell. It is NOT a Bayesian prior strength and carries no claim that inference substitutes for measurement.
dispersion_model.beta_floor: mu*(1-mu)/(nu+1) is IRREDUCIBLE: it does not vanish as n -> inf. Sampling cannot beat it. There is therefore no 'isolates required to reach margin m' — that question presupposes margin is a function of effort, which it is not.

calibration.lambda_definition: nu_cal = nu / lambda^2, with lambda solving <z^2> = 1 exactly by root-find on the COUNT scale (Brent). Replaces the earlier closed-form moment estimate, which acted on (nu+1) and therefore did not achieve <z^2>=1.
calibration.coverage_note: <z^2>=1 holds by construction. Departure of coverage from 95% is a SHAPE effect: standardized residuals are heavy-tailed because nu varies across cells. Matching the second moment does not imply normality.

lambda_heldout_diagnostic.note: lambda refitted on each fold's HELD-OUT countries. DIAGNOSTIC ONLY — never used in any exported column, since that would make shipped uncertainty depend on test labels. Reported so users applying the model to a NEW country know whether the shipped intervals are optimistic.

sampling_effort.framing: nu_cal is the natural unit of sampling effort. At n = k*nu_cal the rate SD is sqrt(1+1/k) times the Beta floor: k=2 -> 1.22x, k=5 -> 1.10x, k=10 -> 1.05x. Beyond ~10*nu_cal additional isolates buy essentially nothing.
sampling_effort.effort_status: n_total/nu_cal < 2 = under-sampled (more isolates still help); 2..10 = at the limit; > 10 = over-sampled (effort could be reallocated to under-sampled cells).

uncertainty_ordering.question: Does predicted variance rank |error| for reasons beyond sample size?
uncertainty_ordering.note: A NULL variance built only from n and the global mean rate attains a high MARGINAL rho by construction, because |error| also carries a 1/n signal. The partial-on-n column and the within-n-stratum rho isolate what the model knows about heterogeneity at a fixed sample size. Compare model vs NULL there, not marginally.

alerts.warning: tau is fitted on each fold's TRAINING countries and is optimistic there by construction. threshold_diagnostic.csv carries train AND held-out AUROC and precision @tau side by side. tau is a logit: its origin is arbitrary, so 'sd exceeds mean' is not a scale-free statement. The tau spread is a symptom of the train/held-out AUROC gap, not an independent finding.
alerts.protocol_split: TWO protocols, deliberately. GATED (tau applied): precision, alerts_per_unit and alert-coverage answer the ALERT question ('how many false leads am I chasing?'), for which tau is exactly the knob that trades recall for precision. UNGATED (no tau): movers-only P@k / recall@k answer the WATCHLIST question ('if I commit to testing k pairs, how much of what is there do I find?'). The k-pair budget is already committed by assumption, so gating could only discard candidates from testing capacity the user has decided to spend — tau answers no question the watchlist user is asking. Do not compare the two tables directly.

metric_definitions.precision: GATED. hits / alerts, over gated top-k, country-years with >= k pairs.
metric_definitions.recall_of_movers: GATED. hits / all movers in the fold, gated top-k.
metric_definitions.recall@k: UNGATED. Mean over country-years WITH >=1 mover of (movers in top-k) / (movers in that country-year). NOT the probability of catching at least one.
metric_definitions.P@k: UNGATED. Mean over mover-bearing country-years of (movers in top-k)/k.
metric_definitions.frac_units_with_hit: Fraction of mover-bearing country-years where at least one mover is caught. Companion to recall@k, not a replacement: it rewards catching 1-of-5 as much as 1-of-1, whereas a missed mover is equally serious either way. Reported in both the gated (alert_coverage_*) and ungated (precision_at_k_movers_only) tables.
metric_definitions.units_excluded_lt_k: Country-years with fewer than k testable pairs. Excluded from the metric, NOT counted as failures.

alerts.rank_columns: down_alert_rank / up_alert_rank: rank within the country-year among pairs clearing that fold's threshold (1 = strongest). -1 = below threshold.

columns.ci_lo_cal / ci_hi_cal: 95% credible interval for the LATENT rate = Beta(alpha_cal, beta_cal) quantiles. This is the irreducible bracket; it contains no finite-n term.
columns.ci_width_cal: ci_hi_cal - ci_lo_cal. What you are left with after unlimited testing.
columns.floor_sd_cal: sqrt(mu(1-mu)/(nu_cal+1)) — irreducible SD of the latent rate.
columns.n_stop_2nu / n_stop_5nu / n_stop_10nu: Isolate counts at which the rate SD is within 22% / 10% / 5% of the floor. Guidance on when to STOP testing, not on what margin to expect.
columns.n_total_over_nu_cal: Current isolates in units of nu_cal. See effort_status.
columns.alpha_raw / beta_raw / nu_raw: Uncalibrated model outputs. Use the _cal columns.
columns.n_context_edges: Observed cells available as context. Low => unreliable; filter on it.
columns.fold_model: CV fold whose model produced the row (never saw this country).

caveats.imputable_cells: No isolate count exists, so there is no ground truth and no n_S_pred. Only the latent rate, its Beta floor interval, and the effort scale nu_cal are predicted; effort_status is undefined (there is no n_total).
caveats.noise_floor: Sampling noise at the median cell (n=93, p=0.77) is ~4.4% SD on the rate.
